author: .fjl
content: For blockchain use, it would also need a way to limit the resources/steps that can be consumed by a call

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: That's pretty easy to implement in Lua since it has a hook that fires every N opcodes, for configurable N. All of the snapshotting and replay is handled by the embedding program

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: Well, the embedder calls the snapshot function; it doesn't need to handle any of the mechanics of it.

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: Which is pretty inefficient since it requires copying Lua's entire heap. I have some ideas for making that a lot cheaper while still providing a mostly-imperative feel, though I haven't decided if it makes the most sense to start with Lua as a base and evolve it or what.

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: I've switched back to the networking aspect for the moment
